name: Punta Lucero, Cima San Roque, Cima del Montaño — circular desde Playa La Arena
region_intro: Jogging around Zierbena offers diverse terrain, from coastal paths along the Cantabrian Sea to trails ascending nearby hills. The region is characterized by its blend of sandy beaches, dedicated bidegorri paths, and mountainous landscapes including Punta Lucero, Serantes, and Montaño. These features provide varied running experiences, from flat, accessible routes to more challenging ascents with panoramic views. Best jogging routes around Zierbena The most popular jogging route is Punta Lucero, Cima San Roque, Cima del Montaño — circular desde Playa La Arena, a 7.8 miles (12.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 43 minutes to complete. This route combines coastal starting points with ascents to multiple hilltops. Another top favourite among local runners is El Puerto — circular in Zierbena, an easy 3.0 miles (4.9 km) path. This shorter route explores the Zierbena port area. Local runners also love the Punta Lucero Battery loop from Zierbena, a 6.7 miles (10.8 km) trail leading through the Punta Lucero area, often completed in about 1 hour 29 minutes. Jogging around Zierbena is defined by coastal paths, sandy beaches, and hills offering sea views. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy runs to more difficult mountain trails. The routes in Zierbena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore Zierbena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Zierbena?
A: There are over 10 running routes around Zierbena, offering a variety of experiences from coastal paths to mountain ascents. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners in Zierbena?
A: Yes, Zierbena offers easy running routes perfect for beginners. A great option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1128476923/el-puerto-circular-in-zierbena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Puerto — circular in Zierbena&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.0-mile (4.9 km) path that explores the charming port area.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Zierbena's jogging trails?
A: Zierbena's jogging trails offer diverse terrain, from flat coastal paths along the Cantabrian Sea and sandy beaches like La Arena, to dedicated bidegorri paths, and more challenging ascents into mountainous landscapes such as Punta Lucero, Serantes, and Montaño. You can expect a mix of paved surfaces, sand, and natural trails.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Zierbena?
A: Yes, many of Zierbena's running routes are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1125006235/punta-lucero-cima-san-roque-cima-del-montano-circular-desde-playa-la-arena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Lucero, Cima San Roque, Cima del Montaño — circular desde Playa La Arena&lt;/a&gt; is a 7.8-mile (12.6 km) loop combining coastal starts with hilltop ascents.

Q: What are some scenic jogging routes with good viewpoints in Zierbena?
A: For breathtaking panoramic views, consider routes that ascend to Punta Lucero or Monte Serantes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14999556" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Lucero Battery loop from Zierbena&lt;/a&gt; offers spectacular vistas of the Bay of Biscay. Coastal paths around Punta Galea Lighthouse also provide invigorating sea views.

Q: Can I find running routes that pass by historical sites or attractions?
A: Absolutely. Several routes offer glimpses of local attractions. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38222421" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Lucero Battery – Playa de La Arena loop from Arena / La Arena&lt;/a&gt; takes you near the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5039525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Lucero Battery&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find routes that pass through the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6747511" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aldapa Auzoa Neighborhood&lt;/a&gt; or along the Zierbena Port.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options in the Zierbena area?
A: Yes, for longer runs, you can explore routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10513408" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gazebo in Santurtzi Park – Monument to María Díaz de Haro loop from Zierbena&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 10.7 miles (17.2 km). The bidegorri path, extending 11 kilometers from La Arena Beach towards Portugalete, also offers a significant distance for an extended run.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Zierbena?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from the refreshing coastal paths and sandy beaches to the challenging mountain trails with stunning sea views. The well-maintained bidegorri and the variety of routes for all skill levels are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any running routes that are suitable for families with children?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' routes aren't explicitly categorized, the flat and well-maintained bidegorri path alongside La Arena Beach is an excellent option for families. Its smooth surface and sea views make it accessible and enjoyable for all ages. Shorter, easy routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1128476923/el-puerto-circular-in-zierbena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Puerto — circular in Zierbena&lt;/a&gt; are also suitable.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the jogging trails in Zierbena?
A: Many outdoor trails in the Basque Country are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in populated areas, nature reserves, or near livestock. The bidegorri and less crowded coastal paths are generally good options for running with your dog, but always check local signage for specific restrictions.

Q: Are there routes that offer a mix of beach and trail running?
A: Yes, Zierbena is ideal for combining beach and trail running. You can start with a run along La Arena Beach and then transition to the adjacent bidegorri or ascend towards areas like Punta Lucero. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32204601" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Lucero Battery – Playa de La Arena loop from Barbadungo itsasadarra KBE&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a route that connects the beach with more elevated terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Zierbena?
A: Zierbena's coastal climate makes it suitable for running year-round, though spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant temperatures. Summer can be warm, making early morning or late evening runs ideal. Winter runs are also possible, especially along the coast, but be prepared for cooler temperatures and potential rain.
tips: A large beach with dark sand, next to the mouth of the Barbadún river. It has sand that stains because it comes from rocks with iron ore.

It was a strategic place during the Civil War. There are four German 150-millimeter Krupp guns with a range of 10 miles. They can be visited through three different accesses, two vertical and one horizontal. The views from this point are spectacular!
With 204 meters of altitude, here you will find pure nature and incredible views!
